error_message: column "posts_monthly" does not exist / column "ai_generations_monthly" does not exist
root_cause: Code referenced posts_monthly and ai_generations_monthly but actual DB columns are posts_published and ai_generations_count in client_usage_metrics table
resolution: Updated all queries to use correct column names: posts_published, ai_generations_count
prevention: Always check actual table schema before writing queries

error_message: column "status" does not exist on deals table
root_cause: Code used 'status' but deals table uses 'stage' column with values: prospect, qualified, proposal, won, lost
resolution: Updated queries to reference 'stage' instead of 'status'
prevention: Deals table uses 'stage' not 'status'. Check schema before querying

error_message: Security advisor: function does not have search_path set
root_cause: Supabase functions missing SET search_path = public
resolution: Added SET search_path = public to all affected functions
prevention: Always include SET search_path = public when creating or altering Supabase functions

error_message: useSearchParams() should be wrapped in a suspense boundary
root_cause: Next.js 14 requires useSearchParams() to be wrapped in a Suspense boundary for SSG compatibility
resolution: Wrapped components using useSearchParams() in <Suspense> boundary
prevention: Always wrap useSearchParams() calls in a Suspense boundary in Next.js 14

error_message: Type 'X' is not assignable to type 'Y' (type assertion failures on Vercel build)
root_cause: Vercel TypeScript compilation is stricter than local. Type assertions that pass locally fail on Vercel
resolution: Cast complex types through unknown first: value as unknown as TargetType
prevention: For complex type assertions, always cast through unknown. Run tsc --noEmit locally before pushing

error_message: Supabase MCP authentication failed / token expired
root_cause: Supabase MCP uses OAuth with browser login. Tokens expire between sessions
resolution: Re-authenticate via browser login when token expires
prevention: Expect re-auth at the start of each session

error_message: Could not resolve host: git.draggonnb.online / DNS resolution failed
root_cause: Gitea domain does not resolve from Windows dev machine
resolution: Use SSH tunnel to VPS and access Gitea via localhost:3030
prevention: Always use SSH + localhost:3030 for Gitea API calls from Windows

error_message: getUserOrg() returns 'User not found' for authenticated users
root_cause: RLS policies prevent anon client from reading/inserting rows when no row exists yet
resolution: Used createAdminClient() (service role) to bypass RLS for user record operations
prevention: Always use admin client (service role) for operations that create or repair user/org records

error_message: TS2352: Conversion of type may be a mistake (Supabase joined query type casts)
root_cause: Supabase TypeScript types infer joined data as arrays. Many-to-one joins return objects but TypeScript sees arrays.
resolution: Double-cast: (data.joined_table as unknown as { field: Type } | null)?.field
prevention: Always use double-cast pattern for Supabase joined query results

error_message: relation "users" does not exist -- all accommodation/email/content API routes returning 500
root_cause: 20+ files queried a non-existent 'users' table. The auth model uses organization_users (junction) + user_profiles (display). No standalone users table exists. getAccommodationAuth(), 13 email routes, 4 content routes, and signup action all had .from('users') queries.
resolution: Replaced all .from('users').select('organization_id') with getOrgId() helper (queries organization_users with admin fallback). Fixed signup to insert into organization_users + user_profiles.
prevention: NEVER query .from('users'). Auth model: organization_users (junction: user_id + organization_id + role), user_profiles (display info), organizations (tenant). Use getOrgId() for org resolution.

error_message: Cannot find module for page: /api/accommodation/* -- PageNotFoundError during build
root_cause: lib/accommodation/schemas.ts was never committed to git. 54 accommodation API routes import Zod validation schemas from this file. Without it, all accommodation routes fail at build time.
resolution: Created complete lib/accommodation/schemas.ts with 50+ Zod validation schemas matching what each route expects.
prevention: After creating new shared library files, verify they are tracked by git (git status) before pushing. Run clean build (rm -rf .next && next build) to catch missing files.

error_message: platform_admin users fail all tier checks (hasMinTier returns false)
root_cause: TIER_HIERARCHY in lib/feature-gate.ts did not include platform_admin. Users with tier 'platform_admin' got undefined (falsy) from the hierarchy lookup.
prevention: When adding new tier levels, update TIER_HIERARCHY in lib/feature-gate.ts

error_message: ERROR: 42710: policy already exists for table
root_cause: First migration attempt added RLS policies for the restaurants table which already had existing policies. Combining DDL + RLS for existing tables in one migration causes conflict.
resolution: Split migration: DDL-only first, then separate RLS migration using DROP POLICY IF EXISTS before each CREATE POLICY
prevention: Always use DROP POLICY IF EXISTS before CREATE POLICY in migrations. For tables that may already have policies, separate DDL from RLS migrations.

error_message: ERROR: column "name" of relation "module_registry" does not exist
root_cause: Assumed module_registry schema columns without checking actual table. Actual columns: id TEXT (not name), min_tier (not tier_required), routes TEXT[], tables TEXT[] (no config_schema).
resolution: Queried information_schema.columns to verify actual column names before writing INSERT
prevention: Before INSERT into shared tables (module_registry etc.), query information_schema.columns to verify actual schema.

error_message: Connection target uses node ID but must use node name
root_cause: N8N connections object keys and target 'node' values must be node NAME strings (the 'name' field), not the node 'id' field.
resolution: Use node name strings as both the connections object keys and the 'node' value in target arrays
prevention: In N8N workflow JSON connections: { 'Node Name': { main: [[{ node: 'Target Node Name', type: 'main', index: 0 }]] } }. Never use id field values as connection keys or targets.

error_message: npm error ERESOLVE could not resolve: peer react@^19.2.0 from react-konva@19.2.3
root_cause: react-konva@19.x requires React 19 but project uses React 18. Locally installed with --legacy-peer-deps which bypasses check but Vercel npm install doesn't use that flag.
resolution: 1) Added .npmrc with legacy-peer-deps=true for Vercel. 2) Downgraded react-konva from 19.2.3 to 18.2.14 (correct version for React 18).
prevention: When installing packages for React 18 projects, always check peer dependency requirements. react-konva major version must match React major version.

error_message: Unrelated histories: master branch (Next.js 16 + Tailwind 4) vs origin/main (Next.js 14 + Tailwind 3)
root_cause: Master branch was initialized fresh with different framework versions. Origin/main had full project history with Next.js 14. Two incompatible codebases.
resolution: Created restaurant-sop-upgrade branch from origin/main, cherry-picked restaurant-specific files from master onto it. Stayed on origin/main's package.json (v14 + Tailwind 3).
prevention: Always branch from the canonical main branch. Never init a separate repo and try to merge later.
